LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER VINCENT MAZZARELLA'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. UNDER THE INDEPENDENT JUDGMENT STANDARD, THE WEIGHT OF THE EVIDENCE FULLY SUPPORTS ALL OF THE MEDICAL BOARD'S FINDINGS OF NEGLIGENCE, GROSS NEGLIGENCE, AND DISHONESTY. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER VINCENT MAZZARELLA'S NOTICE OF INTENTION TO MOVE FOR A NEW TRIAL AND NOTICE OF HEARING THEREON. THIS MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THERE WAS NO ERROR OF LAW IN THE PRIOR DECISION AND PETITIONER HAS NOT SHOWN THERE IS A REAL DOUBT WHETHER THE MEDICAL BOARD WOULD CHANGE THE PENALTY IT IMPOSED. RESPONDENT TO EMAIL A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER TO THE COURT AT CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Intentuion To Move For A New Trial And Notice Of Hearing Thereon
Off Calendar Apr-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER VINCENT MAZZARELLA'S NOTICE OF INTENTION TO MOVE FOR A NEW TRIAL AND NOTICE OF HEARING THEREON.
Appearances: Unidentified counsel appearing on behalf of respondent; no other appearances.
This matter was not argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. There was no error of law in the prior decision and Petitioner has not shown there is a real doubt whether the Medical Board would change the penalty it imposed. Respondent to email a proposed form of order to the court at contestdept302tr@sftc.org. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported.

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Jan-27-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER VINCENT MAZZARELLA'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE.
Appearances: Jessica M. Amgwerd of the Office of the Attorney General (916.445.7376) for respondent and opposing party; Albert Garcia of the Law Office of Albert J. Garcia (510.848.5190) for petitioner and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: denied. Under the independent judgment standard, the weight of the evidence fully supports all of the Medical Board's findings of negligence, gross negligence, and dishonesty. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Dec-20-2011 Continued To Jan-27-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified. Judge: Marla Miller, Court Reporter: Teanna Ward, CSR #11918
